title: pilosella piloselloides subsp. magyarica
intro: Pilosella piloselloides subsp. magyarica, also known as the Hungarian false mayweed, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the dandelion tribe within the sunflower family. It is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which have been utilized in the treatment of wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Pilosella piloselloides subsp. magyarica include its ability to reduce inflammation, soothe digestive problems, and act as an antioxidant, making it a potential remedy for various health concerns. In herbal preparations, Pilosella piloselloides subsp. magyarica can be consumed as a tea, tincture, or salve, often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
